(or Up-frameshift suppressor 1 homolog[[5]](#cite_note-5)) is a [protein](/wiki/Protein "Protein") that in humans is encoded by the *UPF1* [gene](/wiki/Gene "Gene").[[6]](#cite_note-pmid8855285-6)[[7]](#cite_note-pmid9064659-7)

**Regulator of nonsense transcripts 1** (or Up-frameshift suppressor 1 homolog[[5]](#cite_note-5)) is a [protein](/wiki/Protein "Protein") that in humans is encoded by the *UPF1* [gene](/wiki/Gene "Gene").[[6]](#cite_note-pmid8855285-6)[[7]](#cite_note-pmid9064659-7)

## Function

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=UPF1&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Function")]

This gene encodes a protein that is part of a post-splicing multiprotein complex, the [exon junction complex](/wiki/Exon_junction_complex "Exon junction complex"), involved in both [mRNA](/wiki/MRNA "MRNA") nuclear export and [mRNA surveillance](/wiki/MRNA_surveillance "MRNA surveillance"). mRNA surveillance detects exported mRNAs with truncated [open reading frames](/wiki/Open_reading_frame "Open reading frame") and initiates [nonsense-mediated mRNA decay](/wiki/Nonsense-mediated_mRNA_decay "Nonsense-mediated mRNA decay") (NMD). When [translation](/wiki/Translation_(biology) "Translation (biology)") ends upstream from the last exon-exon junction, this triggers NMD to degrade mRNAs containing premature stop codons. This protein is located in both the [cytoplasm](/wiki/Cytoplasm "Cytoplasm") and [nucleus](/wiki/Cell_nucleus "Cell nucleus").[[8]](#cite_note-8) When translation ends, it interacts with the functional homolog of yeast Upf2p to trigger [mRNA decapping](/wiki/Messenger_RNA_decapping "Messenger RNA decapping"). Use of multiple [polyadenylation](/wiki/Polyadenylation "Polyadenylation") sites has been noted for this gene.[[9]](#cite_note-entrez-9)

## Interactions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=UPF1&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Interactions")]

UPF1 [interacts](/wiki/Protein-protein_interaction "Protein-protein interaction") with:

* [DCP1A](/wiki/DCP1A "DCP1A"),[[10]](#cite_note-pmid12417715-10)
* [DCP2](/wiki/DCP2 "DCP2"),[[10]](#cite_note-pmid12417715-10)[[11]](#cite_note-pmid14527413-11)
* [SMG1](/wiki/SMG1_(gene) "SMG1 (gene)"),[[12]](#cite_note-pmid11544179-12)
* [UPF2](/wiki/UPF2 "UPF2"),[[11]](#cite_note-pmid14527413-11)[[12]](#cite_note-pmid11544179-12)[[13]](#cite_note-pmid12723973-13)[[14]](#cite_note-pmid11073994-14)[[15]](#cite_note-pmid11163187-15)
* [UPF3A](/wiki/UPF3A "UPF3A"),[[12]](#cite_note-pmid11544179-12)[[13]](#cite_note-pmid12723973-13)[[15]](#cite_note-pmid11163187-15) and
* [UPF3B](/wiki/UPF3B "UPF3B").[[11]](#cite_note-pmid14527413-11)[[13]](#cite_note-pmid12723973-13)[[15]](#cite_note-pmid11163187-15)
